2e0f83da-cbc5-45e9-a530-d5339eb52bbd.text:
Contemplate one or more meetings and various communications that will involve the disclosure by one of the parties [ DISCLOSING PARTY ] to the other party [ RECEIVING PARTY ] of technical, business,
1(4)
marketing, planning, pricing and other information and data, in written, oral, electronic, magnetic, photographic and/or other forms, including information and data regarding Internet-based transport solutions (Collectively CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION ).The parties agree as follows:

1.

f98b051b-f238-42d4-8e8c-12dfbceee3f0.label: text_chunk
f98b051b-f238-42d4-8e8c-12dfbceee3f0.text:
CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION : AG Projects products and services.

2. Limited Purpose .

  The CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall be used by the RECEIVING PARTY solely for the purpose of discussing AG Projects’ services and products.

3. Property of Disclosing Party .

 The CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is proprietary to the DISCLOSING PARTY and is, and shall remain, the property of the DISCLOSING PARTY .

4. No Copies .

725593c3-4dc1-4965-a766-17421b5b3ca0.label: text_chunk
725593c3-4dc1-4965-a766-17421b5b3ca0.text:
Tangible forms of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not be copied,

 in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of the DISCLOSING PARTY .

5. No Disclosure . The RECEIVING PARTY and its employees, officers,

 agents and affiliates shall hold the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION in confidence and take all reasonable steps to preserve the confidential and proprietary nature of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION , including,

d934b1ae-e862-46fc-88e8-21e5c74b6eaf.label: text_chunk
d934b1ae-e862-46fc-88e8-21e5c74b6eaf.text: without limitation: (i) refraining from disclosing the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to persons within its organization not having a reason to know, and all persons outside its organization unless they have a reason to know and are bound by fiduciary duties of confidentiality to the RECEIVING PARTY ; and (ii) advising all of its employees, officers, agents and affiliates who gain access to the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION of its confidential and proprietary nature.

d6ddf04a-c06b-4c79-a855-8dc338066e55.label: text_chunk
d6ddf04a-c06b-4c79-a855-8dc338066e55.text: The obligations of this paragraph also apply to the fact of the existence of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION , of this Agreement, and the occurrence of all meetings and communications of the parties that involve CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION .

7f798647-86fe-47c1-8f32-949f5ba82dbe.label: text_chunk
7f798647-86fe-47c1-8f32-949f5ba82dbe.text:
In the event the RECEIVING PARTY receives in writing an opinion from its outside counsel that it must disclose certain information, prohibited by the terms herein, in order to avoid committing a violation of law, the RECEIVING PARTY may disclose only that information necessary to avoid committing such violation of the law.

6. Discovery.

fe707866-e1ff-4c7d-9487-08c46ec253bf.label: text_chunk
fe707866-e1ff-4c7d-9487-08c46ec253bf.text: In the event that the RECEIVING PARTY is requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION , the RECEIVING PARTY shall provide the DISCLOSING PARTY with prompt written notice of any such request or requirement so that the DISCLOSING PARTY may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement.

600196cb-67b8-46d7-92df-9dfb74e76c12.label: text_chunk
600196cb-67b8-46d7-92df-9dfb74e76c12.text: If, in the absence of a protective order or other remedy or the receipt of a waiver by the DISCLOSING PARTY , the RECEIVING PARTY is nonetheless, in the opinion of counsel, legally compelled to disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any tribunal or else stand liable for contempt or suffer other censure or penalty, the RECEIVING PARTY may, without liability hereunder,

b8017765-4ba0-4352-af9f-094d72c6707b.label: text_chunk
b8017765-4ba0-4352-af9f-094d72c6707b.text:
disclose to such tribunal only that portion of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION which such counsel advises RECEIVING PARTY is legally required to be disclosed, provided that the RECEIVING PARTY will cooperate with the DISCLOSING PARTY in seeking to preserve the confidentiality of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION before


2(4)
such tribunal.

7. No Use .

ae4abdbc-bd68-48f7-a9f7-0fbc51851b57.label: text_chunk
ae4abdbc-bd68-48f7-a9f7-0fbc51851b57.text: The RECEIVING PARTY understand and agrees that it is not allowed to sell, license or otherwise exploit any parts, products, services documents or information that embody in whole or in part any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION .

25fb8277-d0d1-4b6b-b21e-3520aec65f6c.label: text_chunk
25fb8277-d0d1-4b6b-b21e-3520aec65f6c.text:
The furnishing of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION hereunder shall not be construed as the granting of a license under any patent, patent application, copyright, copyright registration, trade secret or other proprietary right by the DISCLOSING PARTY to any person or entity or as implying any obligation other than is specifically stated herein.

8. Limitation .

 The confidentiality and non-disclosure obligations of the previous paragraphs shall not apply if,

6c8b93f8-c552-4910-8851-986b9f01e613.label: text_chunk
6c8b93f8-c552-4910-8851-986b9f01e613.text:
and to the extent that: the RECEIVING PARTY can prove that the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION was known to the RECEIVING PARTY prior to its receipt from the DISCLOSING PARTY (ii) the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is or becomes part of the public domain other than by the fault of the RECEIVING PARTY ; or (iii) the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is rightfully disclosed to the RECEIVING PARTY by a third party that is legally free to disclose such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION .

9.

b2ff941f-c7f1-4b76-a83f-6699003b9558.label: text_chunk
b2ff941f-c7f1-4b76-a83f-6699003b9558.text:
Return .

 All CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION supplied by the DISCLOSING PARTY hereunder and all copies thereof, in whole or in part and on all media, shall be returned to the DISCLOSING PARTY by the RECEIVING PARTY within thirty (30) days upon demand by the DISCLOSING PARTY .

90dc69c0-1b64-45f5-ad2a-516c6633328d.label: text_chunk
90dc69c0-1b64-45f5-ad2a-516c6633328d.text:
All other information and all copies thereof prepared by the RECEIVING PARTY based on the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION , shall be either promptly destroyed or returned to the DISCLOSING PARTY upon demand, as determined by the DISCLOSING PARTY within its sole discretion.

10. Duration.

48e86352-4571-4b70-a61e-35432191d628.label: text_chunk
48e86352-4571-4b70-a61e-35432191d628.text:
This Agreement shall apply to any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION that may have been provided to the RECEIVING PARTY prior to or after the date hereof, and shall continue to govern the delivery of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION until terminated by written notice from either party to the other, except that the obligations of the parties hereunder with regard to CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed prior to termination shall continue for a period for two (2) years thereafter.

11. Injunctive Relief .

a20ddbfe-9c42-48ce-a50a-3dc68ce3874e.label: text_chunk
a20ddbfe-9c42-48ce-a50a-3dc68ce3874e.text:
The RECEIVING PARTY understands and agrees that monetary damages will not be sufficient to avoid or compensate for the unauthorized use or disclosure of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION and that injunctive relief would be appropriate to prevent any actual or threatened use of disclosure of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION .

12. Liquidated Damages.

d5efb6d6-8e2f-45f1-abad-4b14dedd07ab.label: text_chunk
d5efb6d6-8e2f-45f1-abad-4b14dedd07ab.text: In case of unauthorized use or disclosure of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION , The DISCLOSING PARTY shall be entitled to liquidated damages in the amount of € 10 000 (ten thousand Euro) for each such use or disclosure.

10ca9f87-1005-46ae-810c-737627944ad0.label: text_chunk
10ca9f87-1005-46ae-810c-737627944ad0.text:
Notwithstanding the right to liquidated damages, the DISCLOSING PARTY has the right to take any measures available and to claim and receive a higher amount of compensation if the DISCLOSING PARTY can prove that the actual damage sustained will exceed the amount of liquidated damages.

13. Definitive Agreement.

b36bd502-366c-4c8d-b355-a1564195ccde.label: text_chunk
b36bd502-366c-4c8d-b355-a1564195ccde.text:
The parties hereto agree that no contract or agreement between the parties shall be deemed to exist unless and until a final definitive agreement has been executed and
3(4)
delivered to both parties.

14. Miscellaneous .

 This Agreement shall be binding on the respective parties hereto and their successors and permitted assigns. This Agreement may be modified only in writing signed by both parties hereto.

15. Governing law .

d46a06aa-464d-4742-961b-94be73ddcea5.label: text_chunk
d46a06aa-464d-4742-961b-94be73ddcea5.text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Netherlands, excluding its rules for choice of law. Notwithstanding an action for injunction or other equitable remedy, all disputes relating to or arising out of or in connection with this Agreement shall be finally settled by arbitration in accordance with the Rules of the Arbitration Institute of the Amsterdam Chamber of Commerce. The arbitration shall be held in the Netherlands, Amsterdam and the English language shall be used in the proceedings.

31ea5f1f-aab5-429c-a8df-b4f33387799a.label: text_chunk
31ea5f1f-aab5-429c-a8df-b4f33387799a.text:
The arbitration decision shall be final and binding on the parties and enforceable in any court of competent jurisdiction.

community_summary.0:
**Summary of Relationships Involving AG Projects**

1. **AG Projects and Location**: AG Projects is a Dutch corporation that operates from its principal place of business in Dr. This indicates that Dr. is the location where AG Projects is based.

2. **AG Projects as Owner of Information**: AG Projects is identified as the Disclosing Party, meaning it is the owner of proprietary information that it shares with another party, referred to as the Receiving Party. This highlights AG Projects' role in managing confidential information.

3. **AG Projects and Arbitration**: AG Projects is involved in an arbitration decision, which is described as final and binding. This signifies AG Projects' participation in a legal process that determines the resolution of a dispute.

Overall, these relationships illustrate AG Projects' operational base, its role in information confidentiality, and its engagement in legal arbitration processes.
community_summary.1:
**Summary of Relationships:**

1. **Entities Involved**: DISCLOSING PARTY and RECEIVING PARTY
   - The DISCLOSING PARTY enters into a **contractual relationship** with the RECEIVING PARTY, wherein it provides confidential information under specific terms and conditions.

2. **Nature of the Relationship**:
   - The DISCLOSING PARTY **discloses** and **shares** confidential information with the RECEIVING PARTY, which is obligated to maintain confidentiality.
   - This information sharing is primarily for discussions related to AG Projects' services and products.

3. **Legal Implications**:
   - The DISCLOSING PARTY retains the **right to claim liquidated damages** as stipulated in the legal agreement, allowing it to seek compensation if the terms of the contract are violated.

Overall, the relationship between the DISCLOSING PARTY and the RECEIVING PARTY is characterized by the provision of confidential information under a contractual framework, with legal protections in place for the DISCLOSING PARTY.

community_summary.3:
**Summary of Relationships:**

1. **RECEIVING PARTY and CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION**: The RECEIVING PARTY is entrusted with holding and protecting CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. They are bound by confidentiality obligations, which prohibit them from selling, licensing, or exploiting this information. They must take necessary steps to safeguard it from unauthorized use or disclosure.

2. **RECEIVING PARTY and DISCLOSING PARTY**: The RECEIVING PARTY receives CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION from the DISCLOSING PARTY, establishing a relationship of trust where the RECEIVING PARTY is obligated to protect the information. They must notify the DISCLOSING PARTY if there is a request to disclose any part of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, allowing the DISCLOSING PARTY to take protective measures. Additionally, the RECEIVING PARTY is required to return all confidential information upon demand.

3. **Legal Compliance and Consultation**: The RECEIVING PARTY must comply with legal obligations, which may necessitate disclosing certain information as advised by outside counsel. They may seek legal advice to navigate these obligations and ensure compliance with the law.

4. **Cooperation**: The RECEIVING PARTY is required to cooperate with the DISCLOSING PARTY to maintain the confidentiality of the information shared, reinforcing the collaborative nature of their relationship in protecting sensitive data.

5. **Disclosure Requirements**: The RECEIVING PARTY is only permitted to disclose the portion of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION that is legally mandated, as guided by legal counsel, ensuring that any disclosure is minimal and compliant with legal standards. 

Overall, the relationships emphasize the responsibilities of the RECEIVING PARTY in safeguarding CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, maintaining communication with the DISCLOSING PARTY, and adhering to legal requirements.

community_summary.6:
The relationships outlined in the knowledge graph revolve around an Agreement that establishes critical legal frameworks and procedures between the involved parties. 

1. **Confidential Information**: The Agreement governs the handling of confidential information, setting forth rules and obligations for both parties to ensure its protection.

2. **Modification of Agreement**: Any modifications to the Agreement must be made in writing and signed by both parties, emphasizing the necessity of mutual consent for any changes.

3. **Legal Jurisdiction**: The Agreement is governed by the laws of the Netherlands, which provides a clear legal jurisdiction for its enforcement.

4. **Dispute Resolution**: In the event of disputes arising from the Agreement, they will be resolved through arbitration at the Arbitration Institute of the Amsterdam Chamber of Commerce, linking the Agreement to a structured arbitration process.

Overall, the Agreement serves as a comprehensive framework that not only protects confidential information but also outlines the processes for modifications, legal governance, and dispute resolution, ensuring clarity and mutual understanding between the parties involved.
community_summary.7:
The relationships highlight the interplay between monetary damages and injunctive relief in the context of unauthorized use of confidential information. 

1. **Monetary Damages and Injunctive Relief**: Monetary damages are considered inadequate to remedy the harm inflicted by the unauthorized use of confidential information. As a result, injunctive relief is identified as a necessary alternative to effectively prevent further harm.

In summary, when monetary damages fail to sufficiently address the consequences of unauthorized actions regarding confidential information, injunctive relief becomes essential to safeguard against ongoing or future violations.

community_summary.13:
The relationships surrounding the "Agreement" highlight its critical role in governing the arbitration process and its binding nature on the involved parties. 

1. **Agreement and Arbitration**: The Agreement outlines the arbitration process, specifying the terms for resolving disputes that cannot be settled amicably. This establishes a structured approach to conflict resolution.

2. **Legal Framework**: The Agreement is governed by specific laws, indicating that its terms are subject to the legal framework, ensuring compliance and enforceability.

3. **Binding Nature**: The Agreement is binding on all parties involved, meaning that the outlined terms must be adhered to by each party, reinforcing accountability.

4. **Execution in Counterparts**: The Agreement can be executed in multiple counterparts, which together form a complete document, allowing for flexibility in how the Agreement is signed and acknowledged by the parties.

Overall, these relationships emphasize the Agreement's foundational role in arbitration, its legal grounding, the obligation it imposes on parties, and its practical execution.
community_summary.14:
**Summary of Relationships Involving the Party:**

1. **Confidential Information Protection**: The Party is tasked with the responsibility of safeguarding any disclosed confidential information, ensuring its protection unless otherwise specified. This establishes a critical relationship of responsibility and confidentiality.

2. **Responsibility for Affiliate Actions**: The Party holds responsibility for any disputes that may arise from the actions of its Affiliate. This indicates a direct accountability where the Party must address the consequences of the Affiliate's conduct.

3. **Binding Agreement**: The Party is bound by the terms of an Agreement that outlines its responsibilities and liabilities concerning the Affiliate and other involved entities. This relationship emphasizes the legal obligations the Party must adhere to.

4. **Control Over Company**: The Party can exert control over a Company if it owns more than fifty percent of its voting stock or shares. This relationship highlights the Party's ability to influence the Company's decisions and policies significantly.

5. **Access to Information**: The Party has access to Information as part of the agreement and is responsible for managing this Information, which includes ensuring confidentiality and compliance with relevant obligations. This relationship underscores the Party's role in handling sensitive data responsibly.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the Party's significant responsibilities and control within its network, emphasizing accountability, legal obligations, and the importance of confidentiality in its operations.
